output_summary: profiled datasets: musicbrainz, discogs, lastfm.
This run represents an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was constructed to load XML datasets from three music sources: musicbrainz, discogs, and lastfm.
Schema matching was performed using an LLM-based matcher to align discogs and lastfm schemas to musicbrainz schema.
Blocking was implemented using StandardBlocker on the 'name' and 'artist' fields for all dataset pairs.

Rule-based matching was configured with string comparators using Jaccard similarity on 'name', 'artist', and 'release-date' (where applicable), and numeric comparators on 'duration' with different tolerances per dataset pair.
Data fusion was set up with a strategy favoring the longest string for descriptive attributes and union for list attributes, with maximum used for 'duration'.
Singleton records were excluded from the fusion output.
The fusion strategy predominantly uses the 'longest_string' fuser for five attributes, indicating a preference for the most complete textual information.
The 'union' fuser is applied to track-related multi-valued attributes and 'genre' to combine all unique values.
The 'maximum' fuser is used for 'duration' to avoid zero or truncated values, reflecting a conservative approach to numeric data fusion.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This was an initial draft of the evaluation code for the Music data fusion pipeline.
The dominant evaluation strategy used in this run is a rule-based fusion strategy named 'rule_based_fusion_strategy'.
Core attributes such as name, artist, duration, release-date, release-country, and label are evaluated using tokenized_match, numeric_tolerance_match, and year_only_match functions.
List or set-like track information fields such as tracks_track_name, tracks_track_duration, and tracks_track_position are evaluated using set_equality_match.
Any other detected list-like columns are also evaluated as sets using set_equality_match unless already configured.
Added helper functions to parse source IDs and detect gold ID prefixes for alignment.
Implemented a method to build an evaluation view that aligns fused clusters to gold standard IDs using source prefixes or fallback to cluster IDs.
Included list normalization helpers to detect and normalize list-like columns for robust comparison.
Added coverage diagnostics to report direct and mapped ID coverage against gold IDs.
Configured evaluation functions registration with a compact summary printout.
Enabled debug output to a JSONL file for detailed evaluation traceability.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 40.10%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.
The fusion strategy was changed to use the longest_string fuser for tracks_track_duration, tracks_track_position, genre, and tracks_track_name instead of the union fuser.
The fusion strategy continues to use maximum for the duration attribute.
Singleton retention was disabled in the fusion engine.
The fusion behavior shifted from using union for track-related and genre attributes to using longest_string, aiming to improve the quality and correctness of fused track attributes.
Matching now includes rule-based matching support with string and numeric comparison capabilities.
The blocking method was changed from StandardBlocker to SortedNeighbourhoodBlocker using a normalized block key combining normalized name and artist.
Feature engineering was added to normalize and clean attributes such as name, artist, release date, release country, and duration, and to create a track signature for ordering-aware track fusion.
Matching comparators were updated to compare normalized fields and include track signature similarity with adjusted weights and thresholds.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run performed an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a rule-based fusion strategy named 'rule_based_fusion_strategy_eval'.
Core attributes such as name, artist, duration, release-date, release-country, and label were evaluated using tokenized_match, numeric_tolerance_match, and year_only_match functions.

List-like track information fields including tracks_track_name, tracks_track_duration, and tracks_track_position were evaluated using set_equality_match to compare as sets in an order-insensitive manner.
Any other detected list-like columns were also evaluated using set_equality_match by default.
The evaluation code was refactored for improved robustness including enhanced list normalization utilities with a more robust import mechanism.

The alignment of fused clusters to gold IDs was improved by expanding fused cluster rows into candidate eval_id rows with preference for source ids matching the dominant gold prefix and selecting the best row per eval_id based on fusion confidence.
Evaluation functions were registered in a structured manner with a compact one-line summary printed for clarity.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 46.70%; previous logged accuracy was 40.10%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.
The fusion strategy was adjusted to use the shortest_string fuser for the 'name' attribute instead of the longest_string fuser.
The blocking window size was increased from 25 to 40 to improve recall.
The matching comparators' numeric duration tolerance was increased (e.g., max_difference increased to 300 or 360 seconds depending on dataset pairs).
Normalization functions for titles and artists were enhanced to remove bracketed qualifiers and normalize common joiners.
The block key construction was changed to use a compact set of tokens from the title to reduce noise and key length.
Fusion behavior shifted to prefer the shortest string for the 'name' attribute, while retaining longest_string for most other attributes.
Matching weights were adjusted to strengthen name and artist similarity signals and moderate the weight of track signature similarity.
Blocking strategy was made more permissive by increasing the window size to capture more candidate pairs.
Normalization of release names now removes bracketed qualifiers like '(remastered)' or '[live]' to reduce noise in identity matching.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a rule-based fusion strategy named 'rule_based_fusion_strategy'.
Core attributes such as name, artist, duration, release-date, release-country, and label were evaluated using tokenized_match, numeric_tolerance_match, and year_only_match functions.
Track-related fields including tracks_track_name were evaluated using set_equality_match.
Additional detected list-like columns were also evaluated using set_equality_match by default.
The evaluation view was improved by aligning fused clusters to gold IDs using _fusion_sources when available, creating an 'eval_id' column for better ID matching.
List-like columns were detected and normalized consistently across fused and gold datasets to improve evaluation accuracy.
Evaluation function registration was corrected to match the fusion strategy name and to include only relevant columns.
Code was refactored for clarity and robustness, including improved imports and coverage diagnostics.
The evaluation refinement was necessary to improve ID alignment between fused and gold data, which is critical for accurate evaluation.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 42.13%; previous logged accuracy was 46.70%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.
